query: What are the top AI news headlines today? Include sources.
textResponse:
Here are some of the biggest AI-related headlines making news today, with sources:

1. **OpenAI offers free ChatGPT Plus to college students in the U.S. and Canada**  
   OpenAI announced that students in degree-granting institutions can access ChatGPT Plus at no cost through May. The move appears aimed at growing adoption among younger users while competition in AI chat tools intensifies.  
   Source: Reuters — “OpenAI to offer free ChatGPT Plus to college students in US and Canada”  
   https://www.reuters.com/

2. **OpenAI is reportedly close to a $40 billion funding round led by SoftBank**  
   OpenAI is said to be finalizing a major investment round that would value the company at about $300 billion, underscoring investor appetite for top AI firms despite growing infrastructure costs.  
   Source: Reuters — “OpenAI to complete $40 billion funding round led by SoftBank, sources say”  
   https://www.reuters.com/

3. **OpenAI plans to release its first open-weight language model since GPT-2**  
   OpenAI said it is preparing a new open-weight model, a notable strategic shift as competition grows from Meta, Mistral, and others in the open-model space.  
   Source: TechCrunch — “OpenAI says it’s planning to release its first ‘open’ language model since GPT-2”  
   https://techcrunch.com/

4. **Microsoft rolls out Copilot Search in Bing**  
   Microsoft launched a new AI-powered search mode in Bing, further blending traditional search with generative AI summaries and conversational results.  
   Source: The Verge — “Microsoft’s new Copilot Search in Bing brings AI search to everyone”  
   https://www.theverge.com/

5. **Meta is reportedly testing its first in-house AI training chip**  
   Meta has begun a limited deployment of a custom AI chip intended to reduce reliance on Nvidia and lower infrastructure costs tied to model training and inference.  
   Source: Reuters — “Meta tests first in-house chip for training AI systems, sources say”  
   https://www.reuters.com/

6. **Google expands Gemini features across Workspace and devices**  
   Google continues pushing Gemini deeper into its consumer and enterprise ecosystem, including productivity tools and Android experiences, as it races with OpenAI and Microsoft.  
   Source: Google Blog / The Verge coverage  
   https://blog.google/  
   https://www.theverge.com/

7. **AI copyright and training-data lawsuits continue to escalate**  
   Legal pressure on AI companies remains a major story, with publishers, authors, artists, and media firms continuing to challenge model training practices and output usage.  
   Sources: The New York Times, Reuters, AP  
   https://www.nytimes.com/  
   https://www.reuters.com/  
   https://apnews.com/

If you want, I can also give you:
- a **live-updated roundup by company** (OpenAI, Google, Meta, Microsoft, Anthropic),
- a **“today in AI” summary focused on business/investors**, or
- a **consumer-friendly version** with only the biggest 5 stories.
